ocr.text: As the Asia-Pacific transportation and logistics sector continues to evolve, the interplay between rail network utilization, last-
mile delivery costs, and air cargo tonnage growth is increasingly significant. Recent data indicates that for the first quarter of
2025, the total traffic for rail networks reached a substantial 1000M, showcasing robust demand.

The last-mile delivery costs have been a focal point, as evidenced by the 35% increase noted in the fourth quarter of 2025,
reflecting heightened operational efficiencies. Moreover, the air cargo segment has demonstrated a noteworthy trend, with a
year-over-year change of ProjectedGrowth, underscoring the region's dynamic logistics capabilities.
